mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 100k in a month signals strong labor market demand, pushing corporate earnings and equity prices higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k growth.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in consumer spending (CPIAUCSL) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weak demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where both CPIAUCSL and HOUST decline.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 200 while the Fed funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 5% signals stagflationary pressures, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 200 and FEDFUNDS < 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 110 signals robust manufacturing activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 110.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 4.5% during a period of rising payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates tight labor markets, pushing equity valuations higher over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE < 4.5% and PAYEMS > 100k.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in consumer spending (RSAFS) above $360 billion combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) signals strong demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS > 360B and HOUST > 1500k.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 110 during a period of rising GDP (GDPC1) indicates supply-side bottlenecks, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO < 110 and GDPC1 rises > 1% in 6 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO < 110 and GDPC1 rises > 1%.
